verdict.reasoning: FixedMoonVault.withdrawAll() is protected by the noReentrant modifier, which sets locked before the function body executes and only clears it after the body completes, preventing reentry into withdrawAll() or deposit() during the external Ether transfer. The contract also follows checks-effects-interactions by calling moonToken.burnAccount(msg.sender) before sending Ether to msg.sender, so the cross-contract state in MoonToken is updated before the untrusted call. MoonToken.burnAccount() and mint() are restricted by onlyOwner and do not make external calls, so there is no reentrancy path through the token contract.
